programme.title: Uppercut
programme.desc: Follows Toni's journey from seeking boxing training to becoming a pioneering female boxing manager.

programme.title: Salvable
programme.desc: A boxer struggles to escape the grasp of a small town while battling a broken relationship with his daughter.

programme.title: Southpaw
programme.desc: Boxer Billy Hope turns to a retired fighter to help him turn his life around for one last epic fight.

programme.title: Warrior
programme.desc: Two brothers prepare to go head to head in a mixed martial arts battle in order to win the prize money.

programme.title: Eye See You
programme.desc: Recovering from the psychological effects of witnessing a brutal crime, FBI Agent Jake Malloy (Sylvester Stallone) checks into a rehabilitation clinic for law enforcement officers in the remote wilderness of Wyoming during the dead of winter. He soon finds himself helping other patients as a serial killer begins killing them one by one.

programme.title: Direct Contact
programme.desc: An American prisoner in a Russian jail is given a chance at freedom if he can rescue a kidnapped woman.

programme.title: My Mom's New Boyfriend
programme.desc: After several years away, FBI agent Henry Durand (Colin Hanks) returns to find his mother, Marty (Meg Ryan), a much different person: Having long mourned for Henry's father, she is now embracing single life. One day, while Marty, Henry and his new fiancée, Emily (Selma Blair), are out for a walk, they meet Tommy (Antonio Banderas), a mysterious stranger, with whom Marty then becomes involved. However, through his FBI contacts, Henry learns that Tommy is suspected of being an art thief.

programme.title: The Contract
programme.desc: Frank Carden (Morgan Freeman), one of the world's greatest assassins, is handed a lucrative contract to kill a highly secretive billionaire. But in a twist of fate, Carden is involved in a car accident during the job, and when his gun is found after he is admitted to a hospital, federal marshals arrest him. Meanwhile, ex-cop Ray Keene (John Cusack) is hiking with his son, Chris (Jamie Anderson), when Carden's cohorts try to spring him from custody, and soon the pair is caught in the crossfire.

programme.title: Before I Go To Sleep [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Ever since a vicious attack nearly claimed her life, Christine Lucas (Nicole Kidman) has suffered from anterograde amnesia and is unable to form new memories. Every morning, she becomes reacquainted with her husband, Ben (Colin Firth), and the other constants in her life. In accordance with her doctor's (Mark Strong) instructions, Christine keeps a video diary. As Christine starts to uncover terrifying truths about her past, she begins to question everything -- and everyone -- around her.

programme.title: Donnie Darko
programme.desc: After escaping a bizarre accident, a troubled teenager is plagued by visions of a man in a large rabbit suit

programme.title: Brotherhood Of The Wolf
programme.desc: In 18th-century France, the Chevalier de Fronsac and his Native American friend Mani are sent to the Gevaudan province.

programme.title: A Walk In The Woods
programme.desc: After spending two decades in England, Bill Bryson (Robert Redford) returns to the U.S., where he decides the best way to connect with his homeland.

programme.title: Lake Placid Vs. Anaconda
programme.desc: When they're not chowing down on sorority girls, get ready for the heart-pounding head to head battle between the crocodiles and anacondas!

programme.title: The Fog
programme.desc: One hundred years after their ship was deliberately wrecked, the ghosts of the murdered seaman have returned to Antonio Bay to exact a ghastly vengeance.

programme.title: Robot And Frank
programme.desc: Set in the near future, Robot and Frank is an offbeat buddy movie about an elderly ex-jewel thief and his new caretaker robot.

programme.title: The Throwaways
programme.desc: A notorious hacker captured by the CIA is given a proposition: Work for them or spend the rest of his life in prison.

programme.title: Nothing In Common
programme.desc: On his way up the corporate ladder, David Basner confronts his greatest challenge: his father.

programme.title: Tight Spot
programme.desc: A wise-cracking female inmate falls for a detective who wants her to testify against her ex-boyfriend.

programme.title: The Whole Truth
programme.desc: Movie producer Granger is bored in his marriage to Reed. He has an affair with Canale, a temperamental actress, but decides he prefers Reed's company. After he dumps Canale, detective tells him that the star has been stabbed, and all clues point toward Granger as the killer.

programme.title: You Were Never Lovelier
programme.desc: Love story and musical set in Latin America.

programme.title: Harriet Craig
programme.desc: Harriet Craig runs a household with an iron fist, manipulating others in order to keep her place perfect, no matter the cost.

programme.title: Walk, Don't Run
programme.desc: A middle-aged electronics expert in Tokyo during the Olympics, is forced to share an apartment with a young woman since his hotel suite will not be available for two days.

programme.title: Night Of The Living Dead
programme.desc: A ragtag group of Pennsylvanians barricade themselves in an old farmhouse to remain safe from a bloodthirsty, flesh-eating breed of monsters who are ravaging the East Coast of the United States.

programme.title: House (1977)
programme.desc: A schoolgirl and six of her classmates travel to her aunt's country home, which turns out to be haunted.

programme.title: Sharktopus vs. Whalewolf
programme.desc: When a mad scientist mixes the genes of a killer whale and a wolf, it creates the Whalewolf, and it's up to Sharktopus to stop it.

programme.title: The Poker House
programme.desc: A dramatization of Lori Petty's teenage years spent in small town Iowa.

programme.title: Dominion: Prequel To The Exorcist
programme.desc: Years before Father Lankester Merrin helped save Regan MacNeil's soul, he first encounters the demon Pazuzu in East Africa. Merrin's initial battle with Pazuzu leads to the rediscovery of his faith.

programme.title: Code of Honor
programme.desc: When his family is murdered, a Special Ops operative turns vigilante. His former protégé must try to stop him.

programme.title: Heist
programme.desc: To pay for his daughter's surgery, a card dealer teams up with a co-worker to pull off a casino heist.

programme.title: Marauders
programme.desc: A group of robbers is chased by an FBI agent who uncovers a deeper conspiracy behind the robbery-homicides.

programme.title: Den of Thieves
programme.desc: A cop and an ex-military gang leader clash when the Outlaws plan a heist at L.A.'s Federal Reserve Bank.

programme.title: Man on a Ledge
programme.desc: A police psychologist works to talk down an ex-con who is threatening to jump from a Manhattan hotel rooftop.

programme.title: A Witness to Murder
programme.desc: Sanitation worker MaryBeth is in the wrong place at the wrong time when the mayor's wife is murdered. She refuses to talk to the sheriff she dislikes, but the killers know she was there. Now they're coming for her to make sure she stays silent.

programme.title: 12 To Midnight
programme.desc: A detective gets kicked off the force when the murder of his wife drives him to drink. When a new string of murders seem connected to her death, he soon finds himself squaring off against a killer who appears to be superhuman.

programme.title: Her Deadly Game
programme.desc: When her childhood friend Kayla transfers to her college basketball team, Andrea's reunion turns into a nightmare. Kayla, obsessed with being Andrea's one and only best friend, will harm anyone who gets in her way.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark
programme.desc: A group of virtual hunters unleash a deadly shark in a Texas lake, causing a young couple to fight for their lives during a peaceful fishing trip in a secluded cove.

programme.title: Karma: Death at Latigo Springs
programme.desc: Charley Stevens moves to the town where her recently deceased mother grew up; after arriving, she is plagued with strange nightmares that tease a horrific event, hinting at why her mother left town.

programme.title: Ip Man 3
programme.desc: Martial-arts master Ip Man takes on a crooked developer and his gang of brutal thugs.

programme.title: Ip Man 4: The Finale
programme.desc: Ip Man and his son encounter racial discrimination after traveling to the United States to seek a better life.

programme.title: Master Z: Ip Man Legacy
programme.desc: Defeated by Master Ip, martial arts expert Cheung Tin Chi tries to lead a normal life in Hong Kong until triad leaders draw him back into fighting.

programme.title: Ip Man
programme.desc: The life story of Yip Man, the first person to teach the Chinese martial art of Wing Chun.

programme.title: Ip Man 2
programme.desc: Master Ip, a new arrival in British-occupied Hong Kong, wants to open a martial arts academy to teach his unique Wing Chun style. A corrupt group of martial arts masters, led by Master Hung refuses to allow him to teach in peace until he proves himself as a kung fu master.

programme.title: Husband, Father, Killer: The Alyssa Pladl Story
programme.desc: Alyssa puts her child up for adoption. 18 years later, she seeks her parents out.

programme.title: Beyond The Headlines: The Watts Family Tragedy
programme.desc: Includes exclusive never-before-seen footage and new interviews with Tammy Lee.

programme.desc: Prominent Lawyer Alex Murdaugh's family's hidden crimes begin piling up.

programme.title: Murdaugh Murders: The Movie - Part 2
programme.desc: Alex Murdaugh called police and reported his wife Maggie and son Paul dead.

programme.title: Husband, Father, Killer: The Alyssa Pladl Story
programme.desc: Alyssa puts her child up for adoption. 18 years later, she seeks her parents out.

programme.title: The Single's Guidebook
programme.desc: A content creator making a series about singlehood meets her match in polar-opposite, Jackson.

programme.title: New Dogs, Old Tricks
programme.desc: Liz and Kappa Psi Psi prep for Gamma Fair and T-shirt sales. Alan’s class inspires Anna’s look change.

programme.title: New Dogs, Old Tricks
programme.desc: Gamma Pi Omicron pledges retreat while Pete recovers. Sarah bonds with Jordan, and Allison gets her way.

programme.title: Eat, Love, London
programme.desc: Stella takes a job at a new London restaurant and ends up falling for the chef.

programme.title: Sparks Over Brooklyn
programme.desc: Single mom and chef Lilah is at odds with tech innovator Charlie who buys her family restaurant.

programme.title: The Love Gala
programme.desc: When planning her mom's Garden Gala, Kate must compromise with the head of the Botanical Garden.

programme.title: For the Love of Lily
programme.desc: A career-minded city girl must build a garden with a plant-loving country boy to appease her boss.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Chapman is an ex-marine in Brazil's slums, battling the yakuza outfit who attacked his sister and left her for dead.

programme.title: CODE
programme.desc: Dallas artist MALIK SCHAEFER, also known as CODE, is a rising star in the underground rap scene. Although his checkered past has led him down the wrong road at times, with the help of his best friend, STYLES, Malik is back on the fast-track to success. That is, until he reconnects with the dangerous love of his life, SOPHIA ORTEGA, who threatens to destroy it all. Malik must decide to either follow his heart or live by the code that has served him well for many years: family over everything.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: The Challenger
programme.desc: The Challenger follows the life struggles of a young, Bronx auto mechanic, Jaden Miller. When Jaden and his mother are evicted from their home, he soon finds that boxing may be the key to stop them from living on the streets and the ticket to a better life. With legendary trainer, Duane Taylor, Miller becomes a local hero and captures the attention of a cable network that decides to create a reality show around his goal to fight to the Light-Heavyweight Title.

programme.title: Body Count Bently
programme.desc: Bently has a past for being hardcore. After the murder of her father Jesse and the disappearance of "Kato," Bently hits the streets to find out who killed her father and to clear her name from all of the bodies that are being pinned on her.

programme.title: Bissonnet 2: Back on the Blade
programme.desc: A serial killer is prowling Bissonnet street in Houston, and is targeting prostitutes. Detective Wallace and Benjamin are on the case, and will stop at nothing to bring him to justice.

programme.title: Caught in a Trapp
programme.desc: The story of a young teen who was abused by his father. Following the tragic death of his parents, he grew up on the streets and is forced into a life of running from D Slug, a known drug lord, after selling drugs from his opps!

programme.title: Fist of Vengeance
programme.desc: Ciara Johnson is struggling with life after the death of her father in a local martial arts gym. To preserve her father’s legacy, Ciara is determined to win the regional Wushu Regional championship, and to keep the art alive in her father’s memory. However, she discovers a secret operation that is connected to her father’s death. Witness the showdown between legacy, and the new school of martial arts.

programme.title: Flew'd Out
programme.desc: Tiara is an up and coming social media influencer after going viral for posting a mans reaction online after denying him sex. In an attempt to grow her online profile, she accepts a date from a man named Josiah for an out of town excursion. Over time, Josiah's intentions become more elaborate, deranged, and sinister. Will Tiara make it out alive?

programme.title: The Wrong Someone
programme.desc: Tina and Phillip’s marriage is struggling and teetering on the edge of no return. When her former flame, Mike, comes back into her life. All the while, Phillip begins engaging into a flirtatious affair with her best friend Makella. Will Tina honor her marriage? Or move on to someone new.
The 13th year of the new millenium brings a 13th month, and with it unlocks the gates from hell.

programme.title: Shark Waters
programme.desc: Sharks attack a fishing charter. Shoreline miles away, those aboard are forced to fight for their lives.

programme.title: The Da Vinci Treasure
programme.desc: A treasure hunter is forced into a deadly race to uncover a Christian relic and history’s greatest treasure.

programme.title: The Terminators
programme.desc: A small band of resistance fighters battle the cyborgs that have taken control of the planet.

programme.title: Universal Soldiers
programme.desc: Unstable and unstoppable genetically modified super soldiers escape from their maximum security holding cells.

programme.title: Homebound (Edited Version)
programme.desc: HOMEBOUND is the feature debut and psychological thriller from director Sebastian Godwin, about a father (Tom Goodman-Hill) and his new fiance (Aisling Loftus) whose trip to the countryside, turns frightening when they arrive to find his ex-wife is missing and the kids acting strange.

programme.title: Shaky Shivers
programme.desc: From director Sung Kang (star of Fast X), after finding herself bitten by a mysterious animal, Lucy becomes convinced that she will transform into a fearsome werewolf. Joined by her best friend Karen, the two embark on a campy adventure filled with magic and mayhem, as they look to do battle with a throat-slashing creature ripped right out of an 80s horror movie.

programme.title: Creeping Death
programme.desc: Halloween was always Tims favorite holidayuntil his mother was diagnosed with brain cancer. Now, all he wants is a quiet night in. That is quickly disrupted when his friends pay him an unwanted visit, after stealing a mysterious sack from the doorstep of a local crazy. This sacrilegious act unleashes the Aos Si, a Celtic spirit that walks the Earth on Halloween. They must band together and survive the night or make the ultimate offeringtheir lives--this Halloween, all who trick are treats.

programme.title: The Ward
programme.desc: In 1966, in North Bend, Oregon, the runaway the police capture Kristen after burning down a farmhouse and is locked in the North Bend Psychiatric Hospital. Kristen is introduced to Dr. Gerald Stringer, who uses experimental therapy. Then she meets the inmates Emily, Sarah, Zoey, and Iris and the tough nurse Lundt. During the night and in the shower later, Kristen sees the ghost of a woman and she learns that she is Alice Leigh Hudson, a mysterious wicked intern that has disappeared. When Iris is ready to go home, she is attacked by the ghost of Alice in the basement and murdered. She vanishes and the inmates decide to seek Iris out. Then Sarah is abducted by Alice and killed; the next one is Emily. Meanwhile, Kristen escapes from her room and meets Zoey, expecting to protect her. However, Alice kidnaps Zoey and Kristen runs to Dr. Stringer's office. She snoops his desk and finds a report with the truth about Alice.

programme.title: Piranha (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Mutant piranha escape from a secret research station and head downriver towards a summer camp and a small town's lakeside tourist attraction. A gruff loner and a daffy private-eye race against time to try to stop the creatures.All-new extras and first-time widescreen transfer! First time on Blu-ray! Piranha, publicly acknowledged by Steven Spielberg as the best of the Jaws rip-offs, received the Saturn Award Nomination for Best Horror Film in 1979. Featuring F/X from Oscar-winning make-up artist Rob Bottin (Total Recall, The Thing).

programme.title: Scare Package
programme.desc: Scare Package

programme.title: PG: Psycho Goreman
programme.desc: Mimi and Luke resurrect an alien overlord, inadvertently summoning his intergalactic friends and foes to battle for the fate of the galaxy.

programme.title: All the Creatures Were Stirring
programme.desc: The joyful spirit of Christmas takes one dark turn after another -- from sadistic office parties and last-minute shopping nightmares to vengeful stalkers and immortal demons.

programme.title: Glorious
programme.desc: A heartbroken man finds himself trapped in a bathroom with a strange voice who says he may be the only hope in stopping a terrible event. A SHUDDER ORIGINAL

programme.title: Blood Glacier
programme.desc: It's gore galore in the Alps, as researchers make a gruesome discovery: a giant glacier oozing blood, and transforming all wildlife in its wake into deadly mutants.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Go Hollywood" - When Jake Clampett tells Jed he's his cousin, he's welcomed into the home and soon has everyone but Jed thinking about and dressing for a role in their next movie.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Are Overdrawn" - The Clampetts receive an overdraft from the bank intended for the deadbeat actor J.D. 'Jake' Clampett, while he gets their statement and starts to spend their money.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Chickadee Returns" - Jethro thinks he's in love with Chickadee Laverne, a stripper he met at the bank. She and the Clampetts have meet and have different ideas about what an "engagement" is, while Miss Hathaway tries to avert this disaster.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro's First Love" - Jethro decides that he wants to go a-courtin', so after Uncle Jed gives him a little education, he finds himself a woman--who happens to be a stripper. As usual, neither she or the Clampetts actually understand what the other is really saying.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampette Look" - Cynthia Fenwick makes her filthy rich mother dress in the "Clampett Look" when they visit the Clampetts, since she thinks Elly is a fashion maven. That only reinforces the Clampett's belief that the Fenwicks are poor.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Starts to School" - Mrs. Drysdale recommends Elly for a high-brow finishing school in hopes that humiliating her will drive the Clampetts away; but instead Miss Hathaway convinces the girls at the school that Elly is a fashion trend-setter. Elly invites the filthy rich Fenwicks over, thinking they're destitute.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Garden" - Grannie has everyone up at the crack of down to plow the front yard for her garden, while Mr. Drysdale, Miss Hathaway, and a stubborn mule try to stop her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Hair-Raising Holiday" - After Granny cuts off the top of Drysdale and Clyburn's (his doctor) hair for some homegrown remedy, Clyburn tries to keep her from ever doctoring again. Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Gets the Misery" - Jed cheers up Granny by pretending to be sick so she can doctor him. Soon Drysdale and his doctor, Clyburn, are subject to her methods as well.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro brings home a friend, Armstrong Dueser McHugh III, who is coddled by a chauffeur who treats him as frail. The Clampetts know better, and show him a good time, friendship and family.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Psychiatrist Gets Clampetted" - Jethro finally gets his bill of health from Dr. Twombly so he can graduate the fifth grade, but Twombly really wants to talk to Grannie again. Drysdale brings him to the Clampetts' just as Grannie showed Pearl how to use a love potion, so everyone thinks Twombly's interest in Grannie is love.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Psychoanalyzed" - Jethro needs a health certificate to graduate from the fifth grade, so he goes to the only doctor the Clampetts know about - Mrs. Drysdale's psychiatrist. He gets nowhere with Jethro, so Pearl comes and she thinks he is testing her virtue when he tries to lay her on his couch.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Dan Maury, released from Yuma prison, arrives in North Fork intent on getting even with Marshal Torrance, who helped send him to jail.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas comes to the rescue when escaped convicts hold up the general store in North Fork and take Marshall Torrance hostage.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Young Mark manages to capture a hawk but falls into danger with a rattlesnake. A stranger comes to his rescue and Mark invites the man home, unaware that he is on the run from the law.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas takes in an orphaned teen being raised by his abusive uncle after there is trouble at the school.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: The McCains try to be tolerant of their embittered neighbor Abel MacDonald, but they decide he's gone too far when he won't allow his injured boy to get medical treatment.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: An unfounded rumor causes North Fork's outspoken feminist schoolteacher to be attacked by a mob of angry townspeople.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: A drought has forced local cowhands to seek work on a railroad construction project, but executives are afraid workers will desert them if they believe an old man's claim of making rain.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas McCain gives blood to save a dying man and is surprised by Marshall Torrance's odd behavior towards him. Soon, men arrive from a nearby town and demand custody of the man.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: When Mark finds of the body of a murder victim, Lucas, his friend Artemus and Marshall Torrance begin a search for the killer.

programme.title: The Rifleman

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: When a rancher dies after the bank forecloses the mortgage on his property, his son blames the banker for his father's death.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: When a wealthy rancher is killed, Mark suggests that they ask a mind reader to identify the murderer.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: A group of outlaws devise a plan to get rid of Lucas so that they can take over the town of North Fork.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: After Lucas kills a mountain man in a brawl, his father arrives in North Fork to break his other boys out of jail and take revenge on Lucas and Marshall Torrance.

programme.title: The Last Rebel
programme.desc: A rebellious man and his partner, raise a ruckus in a small town after they rescue a man from a lynching.

programme.title: Last Of The Comanches
programme.desc: After a raid by Comanches, six remaining cavalrymen join a stagecoach and lead it to safety.

programme.title: Feud of the Trail
programme.desc: Feud of the Trail places the dashing Tom Tylerr, a B-Western star (and the original Captain Marvel) in dual roles; as a man seeking justice against a band of outlaws, and the bands vicious gang leader. Capably directed by helmer Robert F. Hill (Flash Gordon, Tarzan the Fearless), and shot at the iconic Lazy A Ranch outside of Los Angeles, the film follows Tom Wade, an honorable man with a face thats nearly identical to nefarious gang leader Jack Granger. Using this to his advantage, Wade exploits his similar looks to impersonate the gangster and infiltrate his band of ruffians.

programme.title: Billy The Kid Returns
programme.desc: Who would ever mistake Roy Rogers for Billy the Kid? That's exactly what happens in this actionpacked horse opera from Republic Pictures. Rogers arrives on the scene shortly after Pat Garrett (Wade Boteler) kills the infamous Billy the Kid (also played by Rogers). Though Billy was a killer, he was on the side of the homesteaders against a coterie of big time ranchers. As Billy's death is unknown to the public, Roy gets Garrett to let him pose as Billy to continue the fight against the ranchers, but without the bloodshed. We also get six songs, including When the Sun is Setting on the Prairie...

programme.title: Bad Man of Deadwood
programme.desc: Roy Rogers and Gabby Hayes are together again in this Republic Pictures gem. This time theyre trying to set up a medicine show in Deadwood, a town known for dangerous entrepreneurs who dont like competition. Sally Payne is on-hand as a member of the troop where Roy and Gabby work as entertainers, while Carol Adams plays a reporter who may have a romantic interest in Roy. Songs include Song of the Dusty Trail, and Home on the Rangeland.

programme.title: Grand Canyon Trail
programme.desc: Roy Rogers and Trigger star in this Republic Pictures feature about the hunt for silver in a deserted ghost town. Songs include 'Colorado Joe' and 'Everything's Going My Way'. Plus, look for Andy Devine as Cookie Bullfincher. Film buffs should look for silent screen foil James Finlayson, a familiar face from various Keystone Cop comedies, as the local sheriff.

programme.title: Colorado Sundown
programme.desc: Murder among ranchers is the subject of this Republic Pictures drama. Starring such names as Rex Allen, Slim Pickins, and Chester Clute, this feature was directed by William Whitney. For Trivia Buffs: At one time Whitney was known as the youngest director in Hollywood, having been hired by the newly formed Republic Pictures at the tender age of 21.

programme.title: Home in Oklahoma
programme.desc: The entire gang is here! Roy Rogers, Dale Evans, Gabby Hayes, and Trigger are all involved in a murder mystery involving the death of a wealthy ranch owner. Songs include I Wish I Was A Kid Again and Cowboy Ham and Eggs.

programme.title: Heartland
programme.desc: A widow with a young daughter travels to a ranch in Wyoming to manage the household of a rancher. After a while the man and woman develop a relationship that leads to a marriage. But life in the harsh place takes its toll.

programme.title: Good for Nothing
programme.desc: While traveling to her uncle's ranch, an Englishwoman is kidnapped by a savage outlaw. However, a dangerous posse mistakes them for partners in crime, and the duo then goes on the run across the frontier to survive.

programme.title: Duct Tape Forever
programme.desc: When an evil land baron's limo falls into a sinkhole outside Possum Lodge, a judge orders the men to pay $10,000 in damages in 10 days or lose their beloved lodge forever. Lodge leader Red Green considers various money-making schemes the men dream up, like selling road kill on the Internet. But it's Harold, Red's nerdy nephew, who comes up with an idea that just might save their wood-smoked bacon: a duct tape sculpture contest in Minneapolis with a $10,000 third-place prize. Before you can say "go for the bronze," Red and Harold are hitching the Possum van to their can't-fail, low-ball entry and heading for trouble. Will Harold get the credit he craves and the girl he deserves (or vice versa)? Is their goose cooked or will it fly?

programme.title: Ranger Gord
programme.desc: Ranger Gord - Such a mystery he is... Explore the mysteries of the forest and the mind of Gord in this thrilling and hilarious episode.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: Glen is blamed for sinking a boat.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon take on the ultimate comfort food by each creating their own version of a grilled cheese sandwich, showcasing three distinct styles that range from classic to elevated. Michael leads with a rich and decadent Croque Madame, complete with a homemade mornay sauce, gruyère, ham, and a perfectly cooked egg on top, sharing tips on building flavor and texture through technique. Carla follows with a Southern-inspired twist, hollowing out a brioche bun to stuff it with pimento cheese and tangy chow chow pickles, emphasizing balance and texture in every bite. Clinton rounds things out with a fan-inspired take on a classic grilled cheese, combining American, cheddar, and gruyère cheeses on rye bread, using mayonnaise for an evenly crisp, golden exterior. Throughout the episode, the trio trades stories, jokes, and cooking insights, debating everything from sandwich ratios to restaurant culture. They later shift into a lively “overrated vs. underrated” game, diving into food trends, brunch culture, and even aging, blending humor with candid reflections. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway and appreciation for sponsors, leaving viewers inspired to experiment with their own grilled cheese creations at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome legendary pitmaster Melissa Cookston for a lively, flavor-packed episode centered around Mexican-inspired smoked chicken wings. The conversation kicks off with playful banter about grilling, live-fire cooking, and Melissa’s impressive barbecue credentials, setting the stage for a masterclass in flavor. Melissa walks the hosts through her approach to wings, emphasizing dry rubs over heavy sauces and showcasing a spice blend inspired by her travels to Mexico, featuring garlic, onion, smoked Hungarian paprika, ancho chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Along the way, the group dives into deeper cooking philosophy—trusting your instincts, cooking with your senses, and understanding that great barbecue is about feel as much as technique. Melissa shares insights on smoking temperatures, wood choices, and why over-smoking can ruin a dish, comparing it to over-salting. The energy stays high as Clinton hilariously spirals into wing-induced chaos, while Carla and Michael debate everything from condiments to air fryers. The episode also highlights Melissa’s new cookbook, Fanning the Flames, described as a love letter to her global culinary travels and the people who inspired her. After cooking, the group digs into the wings, followed by a round of “Chew It or Screw It” and fan mail, blending humor with heartfelt moments. The episode wraps with giveaways and a celebration of community, creativity, and the joy of cooking with fire.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this heartfelt Mother’s Day episode of Chewed Up, Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly reflect on the women who shaped them while Carla leads the kitchen with a show-stopping mimosa brioche French toast topped with frangipane. Inspired by her mother’s love of simple breakfast classics like pancakes and French toast, Carla elevates the dish into a baked, brunch-worthy centerpiece featuring brioche, apricot jam, almond-rich frangipane, and a champagne-orange glaze. As the dish comes together, the hosts share nostalgic stories about their childhoods—from weeknight dinners and Sunday sauces to special outings at places like Bob Evans and Chinese restaurants—highlighting how food connects to family memories. The conversation flows with humor and warmth, touching on generational differences in cooking habits and dining culture. Once the dish is baked to golden perfection, the trio digs in, praising its rich texture and balance of flavors, with Carla’s creative twist winning everyone over. The episode continues with playful segments like “What Would Mom Say,” where the hosts share funny and revealing lessons from their mothers, followed by fan mail and a casual food discussion. The show wraps with a giveaway of Carla’s personal artwork and a sincere tribute to moms everywhere, celebrating the love, lessons, and meals that define family traditions.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes the lead in the kitchen as he prepares a nostalgic Quiche Lorraine inspired by his mother, making the case for a simple yet meaningful Mother’s Day brunch made at home. As he walks through the process—layering raw onions, crispy bacon, and a rich egg-and-cream custard into a par-baked crust—Carla Hall and Michael Symon chime in with their own takes on brunch traditions, ingredient preferences, and the beauty of regional cuisine. The trio shares laughs over everything from vintage baby names to the infamous “Real Men Don’t Eat Quiche” era, using humor to dismantle outdated ideas around food and masculinity. Along the way, they highlight the importance of texture and balance, celebrating the silky, slightly jiggly custard as the hallmark of a perfectly baked quiche. A fan interaction segment adds a personal touch, with a “Fan of the Day” selected for a custom video message, reinforcing the show’s community-driven spirit. Once the quiche comes out of the oven, the hosts dig in, offering real-time reactions and thoughtful observations about flavor, technique, and versatility—debating whether quiche is best suited for brunch, lunch, or dinner. The episode wraps with a heartfelt nod to mothers everywhere and a reminder that sometimes the simplest dishes, made with care, are the most memorable.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon takes a nostalgic trip back to his early culinary days, recreating an OG state fair classic—steak on a stick—while Carla Hall and Clinton Kelly dive into a lively, humor-filled conversation about food, memories, and the joy of eating with your hands. Using flank steak, Michael walks through his simple but flavor-packed marinade of soy sauce, Worcestershire, Dijon, and spices, explaining the importance of cutting against the grain and letting the meat soak overnight for maximum tenderness. As the skewers hit the grill, the hosts debate whether food is inherently more fun on a stick, with Clinton firmly in the “yes” camp and Michael offering a more chef-driven perspective. The conversation branches into strong opinions on kebabs, with the trio agreeing that mixing vegetables and meat on the same skewer is a mistake, leading to soggy results. Along the way, they share personal anecdotes—from Carla being mistaken for Tracee Ellis Ross to Michael’s frustrations with diners’ rigid expectations around classic dishes—highlighting the humor and unpredictability of food culture. The episode wraps with the hosts tasting the finished steak, praising its bold flavor and simplicity, while also announcing a fan giveaway tied to Michael’s Simply Symon show. As always, the group signs off with their signature blend of warmth, wit, and encouragement to keep cooking and having fun in the kitchen.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall takes the lead in the kitchen as she introduces a playful and unexpected dish: fried Kool-Aid, inspired by nostalgic state fair treats from her childhood. As she walks through the process of making the vibrant red dough using Kool-Aid powder, flour, sugar, and a leavening agent, Clinton Kelly reacts with skepticism while Michael Symon shares his own less-than-great experience trying the dish at a fair. The conversation quickly turns humorous as Carla recounts a bizarre childhood memory of having a runny nose at the fair, prompting playful teasing from the group. As the dough is fried into donut hole-like bites, the hosts discuss technique, including how the dough naturally flips in hot oil and how to tell when it’s perfectly cooked. Carla elevates the dish by tossing the warm bites in lemon sugar and offering a Kool-Aid glaze for dipping, balancing sweetness with a bright citrus note. Once tasted, both Clinton and Michael are pleasantly surprised, praising the light texture and more refined flavor compared to traditional fair versions. The episode transitions into a lively fan mail segment where the hosts debate their least favorite vegetables and share personal preferences, followed by a special “fan of the day” moment celebrating a crew member’s mother’s birthday with jokes and a spontaneous song from Clinton. The episode wraps with the hosts encouraging viewers to engage with the show and teasing future fun.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes on a true first in his cooking career by attempting homemade corn dogs without any prior test run, bringing a mix of nerves and excitement to the kitchen as Michael Symon and Carla Hall cheer him on. Inspired by a popular recipe from Natasha’s Kitchen, Clinton walks through the batter and frying process in real time, navigating hot oil, technique adjustments, and plenty of playful banter. As the trio discusses their favorite fair foods—from funnel cake to childhood carnival memories—the conversation veers into hilarious territory, including rollercoaster mishaps and food-related regrets. Despite the pressure, Clinton successfully executes the corn dogs, achieving a golden, airy batter that impresses both Carla and Michael. The tasting confirms the dish as a win, with praise for its balanced sweetness, texture, and classic flavor. The episode also features a “Fan of the Day” segment, celebrating Charlene from New York with a personalized shoutout, along with a quick fan mail discussion about music preferences while cooking, revealing everything from Judy Garland to Metallica and R.E.M. The episode wraps on a high note, with Clinton’s confidence restored, the group celebrating a successful first attempt, and the hosts encouraging viewers to engage, subscribe, and bring the fun into their own kitchens.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon takes over the kitchen to teach essential knife skills and prep techniques that can instantly improve anyone’s cooking game. Joined by Clinton Kelly and Carla Hall, the trio dives into the importance of sharp knives, proper sharpening methods, and why most home cooks only really need three knives in their kitchen arsenal. Michael demonstrates how to properly hold a chef’s knife, sharpen blades using a wheel sharpener, and explains the difference between honing and sharpening, while Clinton enthusiastically jumps in with detailed play-by-play commentary and cooking science facts. Carla keeps the conversation lively with jokes about Michael’s long-winded explanations and practical reminders about time-saving kitchen habits. Throughout the episode, Michael walks viewers through slicing celery, chiffonading herbs, and properly dicing onions while explaining the safest and most efficient cutting techniques. The hosts also passionately debate cutting boards, with Michael launching into a hilarious rant against plastic, ceramic, and glass boards in favor of traditional wood boards. Humor runs throughout the segment, including a conversation about Ginsu knives, microplastics, and Clinton’s grammar-school definition of “interjections.” The episode wraps with the “Fan of the Day” segment, where Lysa from Seattle wins a personalized video message from the hosts, followed by a final round of playful insults and banter before signing off.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall shares her ultimate biscuit-making secrets while teaching two different techniques for creating perfect homemade biscuits. Joined by Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly, Carla walks through her trusted biscuit recipe while revealing the small adjustments and baking tricks she has collected over years of cooking, tasting, and learning from other biscuit makers. The conversation centers around how technique can completely transform the same recipe, as Carla demonstrates both drop biscuits baked in muffin tins and laminated layered biscuits made through folding and stacking dough. Along the way, the hosts discuss the importance of cold butter, the role of steam in creating flaky layers, and why full-fat buttermilk makes a major difference in texture and tenderness. Michael and Clinton enthusiastically jump into the science behind baking while keeping the conversation playful, debating butter pockets, crispy edges, and the superiority of muffin-tin stuffing. Carla also shares clever practical tricks, including using oil instead of excess flour on the work surface and flipping biscuit dough for better height and rise. The trio reflects on how cooking competition shows continue to teach even experienced chefs new techniques, reinforcing the episode’s theme that cooking is a constant learning process. After pulling both styles of biscuits from the oven, the hosts compare the textures and flavors of each version while slathering them with butter, jam, and honey. The episode wraps with Carol from Columbus, Ohio being selected as the “Fan of the Day,” earning a personalized message from the hosts before everyone signs off stuffed and satisfied.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon put eight different store-bought salsas to the ultimate taste test in a bracket-style showdown to determine which salsa deserves the top spot. The trio samples everything from chunky bean-and-corn varieties to smoky roasted garlic blends and fiery chipotle-forward options while debating what makes a great salsa, including texture, freshness, acidity, spice level, and chip compatibility. Along the way, the hosts joke about grammar corrections, “street cred,” and Michael’s love of spicy food while offering surprisingly detailed flavor breakdowns. Carla picks up floral notes in one salsa, Michael compares another to Fruity Pebbles cereal, and Clinton passionately defends smoother, sauce-like salsas that would work well on enchiladas. As the competition narrows, the hosts discover that their favorites lean toward bold, chef-driven flavor profiles featuring cumin, roasted garlic, and layered chile heat rather than basic restaurant-style salsa. Michael gravitates toward the spiciest contender while Clinton and Carla favor a cumin-forward salsa that feels homemade and versatile enough to cook with. The episode wraps with a cheerful “Fan of the Day” segment celebrating Lisa from Detroit before the hosts sign off.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly take on one of the internet’s most overhyped dishes — avocado toast — and each put their own spin on the trendy classic. Michael keeps things fresh and herb-forward with mashed avocado, citrus, chili flakes, dill, mint, chives, and crunchy sea salt while sharing his philosophy on cooking intuitively and using whatever is in the fridge or garden. Clinton recreates a nostalgic avocado and grapefruit salad from the 1980s, layering honey-drizzled grapefruit, poppy seeds, and red onion over avocado toast for a sweet and savory twist. Carla keeps hers simple and flavorful with fresh herbs and crispy shallots. Along the way, the hosts debate food trends, influencer culture, protein obsession, anchovies, and the infamous “avocado hand” kitchen injury, leading to plenty of playful bickering and laughs between Clinton and Michael. The show wraps with the trio agreeing that there’s no single right way to make avocado toast — it’s all about personal taste and creativity in the kitchen.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: Michael Symon kicks off a new era of Simply Symons from Venice, California, joined by Liz Symon and Olivia “Liv” Annacone Cisic for a laid-back backyard cookout centered around the ultimate burger debate: smash burgers versus classic pub burgers. Michael walks through the techniques behind each style, from selecting the ideal fat ratio for ground beef to achieving the perfect crust on a hot grill and cast iron griddle. Along the way, the trio shares stories about old Symon’s Dinners episodes, Liv’s transition into motherhood, and the realities of filming outdoors with planes overhead, lawn equipment buzzing nearby, and neighborhood life unfolding around them. As Michael builds both burgers with grilled onions, pickles, secret sauce, and different cheeses, the conversation turns into a humorous discussion about condiments, cooking preferences, smash burger myths, and burger nostalgia. Liz keeps the energy going with playful jabs at Michael while also preparing his favorite cocktail, the bourbon-based Paper Plane, complete with Aperol, Amaro, and fresh lemon juice. The episode wraps with a tasting session comparing both burgers side-by-side, with everyone weighing in on which version wins the battle. Between the casual banter, cooking tips, and family-style atmosphere, the episode delivers an easygoing and highly approachable backyard burger feast.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon dive into a tailgating-themed episode of CHEWED UP with Michael leading the charge. He fries up crispy Pork Schnitzel Sliders while sharing wild football stories, Carla admits she’s barely tailgated in her life, and Clinton once again finds himself assigned kitchen tasks he may or may not be qualified for. Between talk of The Chew’s abrupt cancellation, psychic predictions, and potato roll “squish” debates, the chaos continues.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton ushers in sweater weather with a decadent, over-the-top hot chocolate while the crew revisits the infamous "Eggnog-gate" story - a stomach-turning behind-the-scenes tale from their network TV days. Cozy vibes meet barely appropriate storytelling.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon kicks off the week with Crispy Baked Chicken Wings tossed in a hot honey and lime glaze. The hosts look back on the freeing final weeks of The Chew before its abrupt end, and Michael delivers a passionate rant against air fryers. The trio introduces their new interactive "Fan Fridge" segment, and Clinton shares a hilarious story about how eating messy wings on a date made him realize he was gay!

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall brings the heat with a comforting Nashville Hot Chicken Mac and Cheese. Carla builds a heavily spiced, vibrant cheese sauce and folds in crispy chicken and large elbow pasta. The hosts reminisce about Carla's famous ground nut stew from Top Chef, discuss their competitive streaks, and debate the merits of retro candies like Peeps and the 100 Grand bar.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave reconnects with a friend he hasn't seen in years, author of the newly released book Smart Mouth and founder of RAMONA beverages, Jordan Salcito. The duo discusses the best wines in the world, the trick to blind tasting, and why Dave might just have to move to France. Dave answers a new Ask Dave about when to splurge on a restaurant, and prepares a braised oxtail dish in response to a previous Ask Dave.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave talks to Ruby Tandoh, bestselling food writer and author of 'All-Consuming: Why We Eat the Way We Eat Now,' about the big topics in food media today. From what food criticism looks like with the new democratization of influencer-critics to the future of recipe creation, Dave and Ruby discuss complicated questions that many in food culture are asking. Dave answers an Ask Dave, then finishes with the first of a two-part ramen recipe by making the noodles.

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Caramelized Milk Chocolate with Plantain Brittle and Sticky Rice & Mango Ice Cream: Adrienne Borlongan of Wanderlust Creamery—one of the most popular (and buzziest) ice cream chains in LA—stopped by the studio to show how she makes two different ice creams: Caramelized Milk Chocolate with Plantain Brittle, and the flavor that put Wanderlust on the map—Sticky Rice & Mango!

programme.title: Majordomo Cookbook Club
programme.desc: Majordomo Cookbook Club - Korean Tangerine Salad with Chile: Deuki Hong and Matt Rodbard stopped by the studio to share some of their fantastic recipes from Koreaworld: A Cookbook! Check out this Jeju Tangerine Chile Salad...a crisp, refreshing, tangy dish with some (adjustable) heat.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Chicken-Bacon-Ranch “Larb”: Andrew prepares a dish from Dinner Time Live that is a testament to ranch dressing. More specifically, a testament to the holy trinity of chicken, bacon, and ranch. This riff on larb is full of flavor and meat. "Riff” because larb is the national dish of Laos — as well as a very popular dish in northern Thailand — and this is by no means an authentic larb. But… is it delicious? Yeah, it is.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Chilaquiles; Warm Artichoke Salad; Ants Climbing a Tree - Andrew makes a delcious Mexican breakfast classic, chilaquiles, and a simple warm artichoke salad, then Chris joins him in the kitchen to make "Ants Climbing a Tree" (Ma Yi Shang Shu), a classic Sichuan dish consisting of glass noodles in a delicious sauce with ground pork and chopped scallions.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Pepperoni Loaf Pimento Grilled Cheese & Everything-Spice Flatbread: Andrew makes Christina Tosi's ultra-indulgent pepperoni loaf, then combines it with David Chang's pimento cheese to put together a crazy grilled cheese sandwich; Andrew recreates David Chang's everything-spiced flatbread—a recipe that started with Chinese bing dough, took a stop in Istanbul, and ended in a New York deli. It's flaky, buttery and delicious!

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Oyakodon & Mortadella Sandwich: Chris Ying cooks a Japanese dish called Oyakodon that he made for his buddy David Chang on a recent episode of Dinner Time Live. Oyakodon translates to “parent and child bowl” and refers to the main ingredients: chicken (the parent) and egg (the child). Chicken thighs and sliced onions are simmered in seasoned dashi, then covered in egg, which forms a custard-y blanket to be served over rice. Chris and Andrew make a Dinner Time Live recipe based on one of David Chang's favorite sandwiches in the world: a cheesy, hot, mortadella sandwich. This version has a twist—a Chinese steamed bun as a tribute to Momofuku's famous pork buns.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Clam Chowder & Spaghetti with Clams - Andrew prepares two recipes from Dinner Time Live: A clam chowder so cozy and good that New Englanders would approve, and Spaghetti with Clams—a simple and delicious pasta dish.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Lemongrass Beef Skewers, Bánh Mì Tea Sandwiches, Spicy Beef Stew - Chris and Andrew make three different dishes from the Seven Courses of Beef episode of Dinner Time Live, including an interpretation of Vietnamese Bo Kho (a spicy beef stew) and Bánh Mì reimagined as a dainty tea sandwich.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Pumpkin Spiced Beef Curry & Popcorn Soup - Chris and Andrew make a delicious pumpkin spice beef curry, based on the dish David Chang made for his guests during the Pumpkin Spice episode of Dinner Time Live. Rather than using duck confit, the guys decided to make it more practical for the home chef by using a big 'ol chuck roast.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Mille-Feuille Nabe & Krispy Kouign-Amann - Chris recreates two recipes from Dinner Time Live with David Chang: Mille-Feuille Nabe and "Krispy" Kouign-Amann.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter - The Vegan Korean Menu: This week, Dave is developing a vegan Korean menu for guests Nikki Glaser and Pete Holmes. Dishes include: Banchan, the Korean name for side dishes served with rice; fried mushroom Tangsuyuk; crispy bindaetteok cones; avocado rice; kongguksu; and Asian pear shaved ice.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Food markets have a strong tradition in the Netherlands and many citizens count on their long street markets for their fresh food supplies. One of the oldest and most famous markets of the Netherlands is the Albert Cuyp Markt, a street market stretching along the upcoming (and "hip") neighborhood of de Pijp.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The Viktualienmarkt developed from an original farmers' market to a popular market for gourmets. The market also hosts a number of traditional and folkloric events. But the center of the social life of the city is the world-famous Biergarten - here the seven historical breweries of the city pour the finest beers of the world.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Perhaps one of the most beautiful and well-organized food market buildings in Europe is the Mercado Central of Valencia. Built on the area where an open-air market stood for centuries, the Valencian style Art Noveau construction is a 'Cathederal of senses'.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The Kapani market is situated just behind the Aristotelous square of the city of Thessaloniki, facing the Aegean Sea. Exploring this market is exploring the city and its history: a tasteful mix of Sephardic, Balkan, Turkish, and Greek delights. All can be found in the intricate streets of the market or tasted at the many cafés and tavernas operating there daily.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: In Greece, they say you can learn to cook, but you're born to grill. Cooking over fire is one of the most ancient ways to enjoy a whole variety of meats, fish, even vegetables. In this episode, Diane visits one of the best-known Greek grill chefs to learn the secrets of cooking over fire. We will see him butchering and Diane will talk about the difference between Greek and US cuts of meat.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: In this episode, Diane visits a local fisherman and fish taverna to learn the secrets of sustainable fish cookery. She discovers some of the local fish and seafood that thrive as well as those that are endangered. She makes kakavia. Back in her kitchen, she cooks up a sea of treats, all environmentally sound, sharing with practices we can all abide by in order to preserve our natural bounty.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Kalimera! That's Greek for Good Morning, and in this episode, Diane explores the varied ways to start the day with Greek-style breakfast and brunch. Diane explores the day's first sips then delves into sweet and savory regional breakfast and brunch specialties, from the islands and mainland. Finally she whips up a brunch cocktail classic, a Mimosa made with traditional Greek ingredients.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: At a local yogurt purveyor in Athens, Diane enjoys a true Greek classic: Greek yogurt with honey. Delving into this simple pleasure leads to a small artisanal producer of Greek yogurt in the mountains of the Peloponnese. He follows the process of making yogurt. Back in the kitchen, Diane prepares scrumptious dishes that illustrate the versatility of Greek yogurt for every meal of the day.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: In a tour of Athens that stretches from a walk with Diane through the modern market to the Ancient Agora with Dr. John Camp. This episode explores the history of this ancient cuisine, making it delightfully relevant to our needs and tastes today. Back in the kitchen, Diane prepares an ancient feast for modern cooks, based on ingredients that have been part of this diet for thousands of years.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: Fish is on the menu when Sara joins Greek Chef Maria Loi harborside near Athens to cook lemony, crispy skinned Branzino along with Beet, Beans and Feta Salad and Lemon Potatoes. Then it's off to Athen's Central Market to buy Retsina at a famous tavern for a rooftop mezze party in the shadow of the Acropolis. Back at home, Sara uses canned sardines for a quick and easy pantry taco.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: In Naples, Sara unlocks the secret recipe for the best Neapolitan pizza at the famous Gino Sorbillos. Fried meat filled pizza may be even better than that, or is the best one a unique star shaped pizza? Just across the bay in Sorrento it's all lemons, all the time as Sara visits a proud family of lemon growers. They make a citrus flecked cheese and Grandma's Lemon Pasta.

programme.title: Mexico One Plate at a Time with Rick Bayless
programme.desc: In their Chicago backyard Rick, and his daughter Lanie, gather the last of the season's tomatoes to make a big batch of Salsa Mexicana, and that is just the starting point for a fast-paced salsa dance that goes way beyond tomatoes.

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: A customer brings in an emotive family letter sent from the Western Front, Steve uncovers the desperate stories behind Gallipoli medals acquired by the team, and Marie investigates the extraordinary history of a canine bravery medal.

programme.title: Mythical Roads
programme.desc: Upon landing in Mexico centuries ago the Spanish immediately found silver. The Spanish then constructed a road 3,000 km long to transport the silver from the mines, a road full of hopes, but also riddled with pitfalls and danger. Explore this ancient road from Mexico to the southern United States.

programme.title: Inside America's National Parks
programme.desc: Experience the supreme and sacred spectacle of the Grand Canyon. This insider's view of one of the world's greatest natural wonders takes you rim-to-rim to hike hidden canyons, plunge into ancient pools, raft world-class rapids, discover manmade gems, and explore extreme backcountry.

programme.title: Great Destinations
programme.desc: Toronto is a bright, bustling cosmopolitan city whose residents have roots across the globe. You will find everything in Toronto, including world-class food, art, culture, beaches, museums, nightlife, theaters, and the beautiful outdoors.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Inspired by a family story, Ross travels to Scapa Flow in the Orkney Islands to explore the F2, a captured German scout warship whose contents might provide a link to his relatives. Ross also dives the SS Tabarka, a Royal Navy freighter.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Divemaster Neil Brock acquaints Ross with the dangers of becoming a deep-sea treasure hunter. Ross is then given the extraordinary opportunity to be among the few divers allowed to dive the Mary Rose, Britain's most famous shipwreck.

programme.title: Music Voyager
programme.desc: We begin our journey in the Atlas Mountains before traveling onward to Fes, Khenifra, and Merzouga. As the topography changes, notice how the people, culture, and music also change as well. Experience the Sahara Desert under the stars.

programme.title: Music Voyager
programme.desc: Lafayette is the epicenter of Cajun and Creole culture, a place where infectious rhythms of zydeco and Cajun music echo across the bayou. Settle in with Music Voyager and experience the food, lifestyles, and culture of Southwest Louisiana.

Billy does some quick thinking...and his skateboard might just go through the car wash after all!
